message.content:
Adversarial reviewer, HC-GRC hardening batch 19 (pass-8 fixes). Repo /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc, branch hardening/pass-8, UNCOMMITTED working tree. UNIT: pyproject dev bounds
FILES: pyproject.toml
INTENT: pytest/pytest-cov dev extras get <9 / <6 ceilings to match requirements-ci.txt; ruff/black/pre-commit left floor-only (tooling, not in requirements-ci.txt).
VERIFY (try to refute): Confirm only pytest/pytest-cov were bounded, bounds match requirements-ci.txt, and nothing else regressed.

Read the real code read-only. Cite file:line. resolves=false / introduces_regression=true on a real problem.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Now let me check the requirements-ci.txt file to verify the bounds match:

content.content:
1	# Minimal dependencies for CI test runs (Phase 0 tests only).
2	# Does NOT include the full ML stack (PyTorch, transformers, etc.) — those are
3	# Phase 1 execution dependencies. CI runs the structural/protocol tests.
4	#
5	# Versions are bounded to the same major as requirements.txt so CI exercises the
6	# versions production runs on (pass-2 #8/#191). The Tier-2/Tier-3 LLM clients
7	# (langchain-ollama, claude-agent-sdk) are intentionally omitted: the suite runs
8	# with HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING=1 and imports them lazily, so they are never needed.
9	langgraph>=1.2,<2
10	langgraph-checkpoint-postgres>=3.1,<4
11	# Pin the checkpoint core to the exact floor the postgres saver needs
12	# (langgraph-checkpoint-postgres 3.1 requires >=4.1,<5) so CI and production resolve
13	# the same version — parity (#260, pass-2 #191; floor corrected pass-6).
14	langgraph-checkpoint>=4.1,<5
15	# psycopg (pool + binary) backs the pooled PostgresSaver now that the checkpointer
16	# durability tests run against a Postgres service container in CI (#260). Floor
17	# matches the production requirements.txt (>=3.2) so CI exercises the same minor
18	# the node runs, not an older 3.1 (pass-5).
19	psycopg[binary,pool]>=3.2,<4
20	langchain-core>=1.4,<2
21	# reasoning_client imports the OTel API at module load; config loads YAML.
22	opentelemetry-sdk>=1.25,<2
23	PyYAML>=6.0,<7
24	# Match the production floors (requirements.txt) so CI exercises the same major
25	# versions that run on the py3.14 node, not stale ones (#264).
26	numpy>=2.1,<3
27	scikit-learn>=1.5,<2
28	# Major-version ceilings so CI runs the same major as production (requirements.txt
29	# pins pytest==8.2.2 / pytest-cov==5.0.0); without these, pip resolves pytest to
30	# 9.x and CI no longer exercises the version the node runs (#264 pattern, pass-5).
31	pytest>=8.2.2,<9
32	pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6
33

content.text: Excellent. Now let me verify that only these two packages were changed and nothing else regressed by checking the diff of the other modified files:

content.content:
diff --git a/src/reasoning_client.py b/src/reasoning_client.py
index 131d786..06201fc 100644
--- a/src/reasoning_client.py
+++ b/src/reasoning_client.py
@@ -105,10 +105,13 @@ T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
 # Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
 # negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
 MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
-BACKOFF_BASE = float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5"))
+# Clamp the backoff bases >= 0 too (like MAX_RETRIES): a negative override would feed
+# a negative argument to time.sleep(), which raises ValueError mid-retry — uncaught,
+# crashing the call (pass-8).
+BACKOFF_BASE = max(0.0, float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5")))
 # Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
 # fail fast onto a metered path.
-RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30"))
+RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = max(0.0, float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30")))
 RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
 
 # Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
@@ -751,6 +754,11 @@ def is_available(tier: Tier) -> bool:
     if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
         return False
     if tier is Tier.T2:
+        # ADR-0002: even the liveness probe must not hit a non-loopback endpoint —
+        # guard T2_BASE_URL here too, mirroring _t2_complete (pass-8). Fail loud on
+        # a misconfigured remote host rather than silently probing it off-box.
+        from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
+        require_local_uri(T2_BASE_URL, "Tier-2 Ollama (HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL)")
         # Hit Ollama's /api/tags — no generation, short timeout — and confirm the
         # configured model is actually pulled, not merely that the server is up (#210).
         import json as _json
diff --git a/tests/test_reasoning_client.py b/tests/test_reasoning_client.py
index aa26203..6f01c5f 100644
--- a/tests/test_reasoning_client.py
+++ b/tests/test_reasoning_client.py
@@ -447,6 +447,40 @@ def test_is_available_t3_requires_cli_and_creds(monkeypatch):
     assert is_available(Tier.T3) is True
 
 
+def test_is_available_t2_rejects_non_loopback_base_url(monkeypatch):
+    # pass-8: the liveness probe must enforce the same ADR-0002 loopback guard as
+    # _t2_complete — don't even probe a remote Ollama endpoint.
+    from src.infrastructure import config
+    monkeypatch.delenv("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", raising=False)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "T2_BASE_URL", "http://10.0.0.9:11434")
+    with pytest.raises(config.ConfigError, match="not loopback"):
+        is_available(Tier.T2)
+
+
+def test_backoff_bases_clamped_non_negative():
+    # pass-8: a negative backoff override must be clamped at config load, else
+    # time.sleep(negative) raises an uncaught ValueError mid-retry. Verified in a
+    # FRESH interpreter (env set before import) to avoid in-process module-reload
+    # contamination of other tests.
+    import os
+    import subprocess
+    import sys
+    from pathlib import Path
+
+    repo = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+    code = (
+        "import src.reasoning_client as r; "
+        "assert r.BACKOFF_BASE >= 0.0, r.BACKOFF_BASE; "
+        "assert r.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF >= 0.0, r.RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF; "
+        "print('ok')"
+    )
+    env = {**os.environ, "HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF": "-5", "HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF": "-30"}
+    result = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-c", code], cwd=str(repo),
+                            capture_output=True, text=True, env=env)
+    assert result.returncode == 0, result.stderr
+    assert "ok" in result.stdout
+
+
 def test_is_available_t2_handles_network_errors(monkeypatch):
     # pass-6: a wedged/absent Ollama must degrade to False, not raise — covers the
     # urlopen-raises, non-200, and malformed-JSON branches.

content.text: Excellent match. Now let me verify that the tooling packages (ruff, black, pre-commit) were NOT modified:
And the current version:

Perfect. The tooling packages remain unchanged (floor-only). Let me now create a summary of my findings.

## Analysis Summary

**File Analyzed:** /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/pyproject.toml

**Changes Made:**
1. **pytest:** Changed from `"pytest>=8.2.2"` to `"pytest>=8.2.2,<9"` (line 30)
2. **pytest-cov:** Changed from `"pytest-cov>=5.0.0"` to `"pytest-cov>=5.0.0,<6"` (line 31)
3. **Added explanatory comment** (lines 27-29) explaining the rationale for the ceilings

**Verification Results:**

✓ **Only pytest and pytest-cov were bounded:** Confirmed. The tooling packages (ruff, black, pre-commit) remain floor-only with no ceiling bounds added.

✓ **Bounds match requirements-ci.txt exactly:**
  - pytest: `>=8.2.2,<9` matches requirements-ci.txt line 31
  - pytest-cov: `>=5.0.0,<6` matches requirements-ci.txt line 32

✓ **No other sections regressed:** The diff shows exactly one hunk touching only lines 24-35 in the `[project.optional-dependencies]` dev section. No changes to:
  - build-system
  - project metadata
  - other optional-dependencies
  - tool configurations (black, ruff, pytest.ini_options)

✓ **Rationale is sound:** The comment correctly explains that these ceilings ensure [dev] installs resolve to the same major versions as requirements-ci.txt, preventing pytest from drifting to 9.x as mentioned in the comment and the issue reference (#264, pass-8).

**No regressions detected.** This change is precisely scoped and correctly implemented.

input.findings: VERIFIED: pytest and pytest-cov dev dependencies now have major-version ceilings matching requirements-ci.txt. pytest: >=8.2.2,<9 (pyproject.toml:30, requirements-ci.txt:31); pytest-cov: >=5.0.0,<6 (pyproject.toml:31, requirements-ci.txt:32). Tooling packages (ruff>=0.4.8, black>=24.4.2, pre-commit>=3.5) remain floor-only as intended, since they are not in requirements-ci.txt. input.recommended_action: Accept - changes are correctly scoped, bounds match requirements-ci.txt exactly, and no unintended modifications detected.
